title: How Some Frogs Survive Being Frozen Solid in Winter

lesson.learn:
Some frogs, especially wood frogs, can survive winter by freezing part of their bodies. Their hearts stop, ice forms in tissues outside their cells, and special chemicals protect delicate structures from damage. When temperatures rise in spring, they thaw and hop away as if nothing happened.

That is not the same as being dead and coming back. It is a carefully evolved survival state that lets the frog pause life until the weather improves.
lesson.simpler: Wood frogs can survive freezing temperatures because their bodies make protective chemicals that keep their cells from being damaged by ice.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Freeze tolerance lets some frogs pause most of their biology until spring returns.

learnImageCredit.creator: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service - Midwest Region

thirdImageCredit.creator: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service - Midwest Region

lesson.deeperDive:
The trick is controlling where ice forms. If ice stays outside the cells and the tissues are protected by cryoprotectants like glucose, the frog can survive a frozen state that would kill most animals. This adaptation works because the frog's chemistry changes with the seasons. The body prepares for freezing before winter actually hits.

Freeze tolerance is one of the most astonishing examples of seasonal adaptation. It shows that life can survive conditions that seem impossible, as long as evolution has had enough time to build the right chemistry.
lesson.funFact: A frozen wood frog can restart in spring.
references: US Fish and Wildlife Service - amphibian adaptations

people: Herpetologists - scientists who study frogs and reptiles
Physiologists - experts in how bodies function
Wildlife biologists - people who study frog habitats
places: Boreal forests - places where freeze tolerance matters
Vernal pools - breeding waters for wood frogs
Northern wetlands - habitats with harsh winters
Field study sites - where seasonal behavior is observed
years: Late 20th century - freeze tolerance becomes a major research topic
21st century - molecular studies explain the chemistry
Present day - the adaptation remains a model for cryobiology
Each winter - frogs repeat the survival cycle
concepts: Freeze tolerance - surviving internal freezing without death
Cryoprotectant - a chemical that protects cells during freezing
Metabolism - the chemical processes that keep an organism alive
Seasonal adaptation - changes tied to the time of year
Thermal stress - damage caused by extreme cold or heat
